document.masterDocTitle: The genus Drymeia Meigen in the Caucasus Mountains, with a note on the identity of Aspilia glacialis Rondani, 1866 (Diptera: Muscidae)

mods:titleInfo.mods:title: The genus Drymeia Meigen in the Caucasus Mountains, with a note on the identity of Aspilia glacialis Rondani, 1866 (Diptera: Muscidae)

subSubSection.paragraph: Diagnosis. The male of this species can be easily recognised by the densely haired eyes.

Material Examined.
GEORGIA : Mtskheta-Mtianeti region : meadows E of Kazbegi [~ 42°39’N 44°38’E ],

paragraph.treatmentCitationGroup:
D. vicana
is widely distributed in Armenia and was recorded at altitudes from
1870 to 2270 m
in the provinces of Aragatsotn , Gegharkunik , Kotayk , Tavush and Vayots Dzor by
Pont (2018: 15)
.

subSubSection.paragraph: Remarks. This is a common and widespread species in lowland and submontane Europe and is found as far east as the Magadan Province of the Russian Far East. Adult females are common sweat-flies.
